title: Cleopatra's Real Origins
fact: Cleopatra VII was actually of Macedonian Greek origin, not ethnically Egyptian, as she belonged to the Ptolemaic dynasty.

title: The Lost City of Pompeii
fact: Pompeii was buried under volcanic ash from Mount Vesuvius in 79 AD, preserving buildings and even food in incredible detail.

title: Barbanegra's Fear Tactics
fact: Edward Teach, known as Blackbeard, used burning fuses in his beard during battle to appear like a demon.

title: Machu Picchu Mystery
fact: Machu Picchu was likely built as a royal estate for Inca emperor Pachacuti in the 15th century.

title: The Colosseum Traps
fact: The Roman Colosseum had underground tunnels called 'hypogeum' used to lift animals and gladiators into the arena.

title: Nazca Lines Mystery
fact: The Nazca Lines are giant geoglyphs carved into the desert, visible only from the air.

title: Cleopatra's Death Myth
fact: Popular belief says Cleopatra died from an asp bite, but some historians suggest poison was more likely.

title: The Roman Roads
fact: The Roman Empire built over 400,000 km of roads, many of which still exist today in modern Europe.

title: Inca Quipu System
fact: The Inca used knotted strings called quipus to record numbers and possibly narrative information.

title: Ancient Egyptian Medicine
fact: Egyptians used honey as an antibacterial treatment for wounds thousands of years ago.

title: Coordinate 29.9792° N, 31.1342° E
fact: Did you know that here you can find the Great Pyramid of Giza, which was the tallest man-made structure in the world for over 3,800 years and is perfectly aligned with true north?

title: Coordinate 51.1789° N, 1.8262° W
fact: Did you know that here you can find Stonehenge, a prehistoric monument where some of the smaller 'bluestones' were transported from over 200 kilometers away in Wales?

title: Coordinate 20.6843° N, 88.5678° W
fact: Did you know that here you can find the Temple of Kukulcan at Chichen Itza, where during the equinox, the sunlight creates the visual illusion of a serpent crawling down the staircase?

title: Coordinate 30.3285° N, 35.4444° E
fact: Did you know that here you can find the city of Petra, an entire archaeological city where the temples and tombs are carved directly into pink sandstone cliff faces?

title: Coordinate 40.4191° N, 15.0053° E
fact: Did you know that here you can find the Temples of Paestum, some of the best-preserved ancient Greek temples in the world, located surprisingly in modern-day Italy?
